annotations.description: Value that uniquely identifies the purchase order.
Number that uniquely identifies the purchase order in the sold-to legal entity.
Value that uniquely identifies the legal entity that's financially responsible for purchases on the purchase order.
Party financially responsible for purchases on the purchase order.
Abbreviation that identifies the purchase order status in the order life cycle.
Status of the purchase order relative to the purchase order life cycle.
Value that uniquely identifies the business unit that manages and owns the purchase order.
Business unit that manages and owns the purchase order.
Value that uniquely identifies the business unit that creates the requisition.
Business unit that creates the requisition.
Value that uniquely identifies the business unit that processes supplier invoices for the purchase order.
Business unit that processes supplier invoices for the purchase order.
Versions of the purchase order that requires communication to and, as an option, get acknowledgment from the supplier.
Value that uniquely identifies the buyer.
Person responsible for managing the purchase order.
Value that uniquely identifies the supplier.
Supplier who fulfills the item.
Value that uniquely identifies the party for the supplier.
Value that uniquely identifies the supplier site.
Location that the supplier uses to purchase the item.
Value that uniquely identifies the supplier contact.
Person who represents the supplier when communicating with the buyer.
Value that uniquely identifies the bill-to location.
Bill-to location where the supplier must send the invoice.
Rate type that the application uses to determine the currency conversion rate for the purchase order.
Total of the amounts from the purchase order lines. Doesn't include exclusive taxes.
Total of the amounts from the purchase order lines. doesn't include credits, discounts, or exclusive taxes.
Total of the amounts from the purchase order lines with a credit line type.
Total of the discount amounts from the purchase order lines.
Abbreviation that identifies the purchase order currency.
Purchase order currency.
Exclusive tax amount that the purchase can recover and not recover for the purchase order.
Ordered amount plus the exclusive tax amounts for the purchase order.
Description of the purchase order.

Value that uniquely identifies the contract from enterprise contracts. This attribute allows a direct connection between execution documents in Oracle Purchasing Cloud and high-level contracts in Oracle Enterprise Contracts Cloud.

Number that identifies the contract from enterprise contracts. This attribute allows a direct connection between execution documents in Oracle Purchasing Cloud and high-level contracts in Oracle Enterprise Contracts Cloud.

Value that uniquely identifies the contract type. This attribute specifies the nature of the enterprise contract. The contract type specifies what kind of information you can enter including the presence of lines and contract terms.

Type of contract. This attribute specifies the nature of the enterprise contract. The contract type specifies what kind of information you can enter including the presence of lines and contract terms.
Abbreviation that identifies the acknowledgment that the purchase order requires from the supplier. Values include Y for document and schedule, D for document, or N for none.

Acknowledgment that the application requires from the supplier. Values include Document and Schedule when supplier must acknowledge the header and schedule of the purchase order, Document when supplier must acknowledge the header of the purchase order, or None when the purchase order doesn't require acknowledgment.
Document number that the supplier created for the purchase order.
Document number of the purchase order that the import imported from the legacy application into Oracle Purchasing Cloud.
Date that the application requests the supplier to acknowledge the purchase order.
Value that uniquely identifies the payment terms.
Terms that the application uses to schedule payments and calculate due dates, discount dates, and discount amounts for each invoice against the purchase order.
Value that uniquely identifies the company that transports the item through land, sea, or air.
Company that transports the item through land, sea, or air.
Abbreviation that identifies the mode of transport.
Mode of transport that the shipper uses to transport the item, such as land, sea, or air.
Abbreviation that identifies the service level for the shipping method.
Priority that determines how quickly to transport the item.
Transportation method for the carrier or service level for the purchase order. For example, ABC Shipping - AIR - 2nd Day.
Abbreviation that identifies the type of freight terms for the purchase order.
Terms that describe who pays charges to transport the item.
Abbreviation that identifies the type of term for free on board for the item.
Free on board. Location where ownership of the item transfers from the supplier to the buyer.
Contains one of the following values: true or false. If true, then the purchase order requires signatures to meet legal requirements. If false, then the purchase order doesn't require signatures.
Number of days the supplier can decide to accept or reject the purchase order.

Contains one of the following values: true or false. If true, then the buyer or an agent that the buyer designates must arrange transportation, from picking up the requested item to delivering it to the ship-to location that the purchase order specifies. If false, then the supplier must arrange transportation.

Contains one of the following values: true or false. If true, then the application creates an invoice that requests payment for the item according to the receipt transaction. If false, then the application doesn't require the supplier to create this invoice and instead requests payment according to the receipt of the shipped item.

Contains one of the following values: true or false. If true, then the document confirms that the supplier is aware of the purchase order. If false, then the supplier isn't aware of the purchase order.
Note that the supplier can read to get details about how to process the purchase order.
Note that the receiver can read to get details about how to process the purchase order.
Number that uniquely identifies the current version of the purchase order.
Date and time when the buyer or application creates the purchase order.
User who created the purchase order.
Date and time when the buyer or application most recently updated the purchase order.
User who most recently updated the purchase order.
Value that indicates the source application from which the purchase order was imported.
Value that uniquely identifies the user specified on the purchase order as the overriding approver for approval routing.
Overriding first approver identifier to route purchase orders for approval.
Method by which the purchase order is communicated to the supplier, such as email or fax.
Email address to which a copy of the purchase order communication email is sent without letting other recipients know.
Email address of the supplier for communication.
Fax number used to communicate the purchase order to the supplier.
Email address to which a copy of the purchase order communication email is sent.

Contains one of the following values: true or false. If true, the purchase order can't be communicated using B2B communication, and you can communicate it using an alternate communication method such as email. If false, the purchase order must be communicated using B2B communication when the supplier site is configured to receive B2B messages.
Email addresses of additional supplier contacts. These email addresses are used to send purchase orders to supplier contacts involved in the procurement process.
Type, such as bill-only, that describes the behavior of purchasing documents that need to be handled in a special manner.
Abbreviation that identifies the special handling type.
Conversion rate to use when converting the currency.
Date to use for the conversion rate when converting an ordered amount into another currency. The conversion rate varies depending on the date.
Number that identifies the requisition.
Number that uniquely identifies the source agreement that the purchase order line references.
Identifies the sales order that's to be fulfilled by the material being procured.
Number that uniquely identifies the negotiation that the purchase order line references.
Value that uniquely identifies the common internal or external location where the supplier ships all the items.
Abbreviation that identifies the location when items are shipped to an internal location.
Value that identifies the common internal or external address where the supplier ships all the items.
Value that uniquely identifies the deliver-to person or requester.
Name of the deliver-to person or requester if unique among all distributions, else Multiple.
Display name of the person responsible for managing the purchase order.
Method by which the purchase order is communicated to the supplier, such as email or fax.
Display name of the person who represents the supplier when communicating with the buyer.
Value that uniquely identifies the purchasing style of the document.

Contains one of the following values: true or false. If true, then the progress payment is enabled for the style. If false, then the progress payment isn't enabled for the style. This attribute doesn't have a default value.
Value that indicates whether the line is for goods or services.
Value that identifies the purchasing document style.
Name of the user specified on the purchase order as the overriding approver for the approval routing.
Contains one of the following values: true or false. If true, then the document style supports consignment. If false, then the document style doesn't support consignment.
Contains one of the following values: true or false. If true, then the purchase order is frozen and modifications aren't allowed. If false, then the purchase order isn't frozen.
Code identifying the status of the funds associated with the purchase order.
Value indicating if budgetary control is enabled for a purchase order.
Date when the purchase order was communicated to the supplier.
Abbreviation that identifies the document fiscal classification.
Value that uniquely identifies the document fiscal classification.
Tax classification used by a tax authority to categorize a document.
Value that uniquely identifies the first-party registration number.
Value that's a unique sequence of letters or numbers assigned to a first party by a tax authority when the first party is registered. This attribute is used to identify the first-party registration.
Value that uniquely identifies the third-party registration.

Value that's a unique sequence of letters or numbers assigned to a third party or third-party site by a tax authority. This attribute is used to identify the party or party-site level tax registration.
Abbreviation that identifies the country for taxation.
Country where the transaction takes place. This attribute is used for taxation.
Value that identifies the bill-to location address to which the supplier must send the invoice.

Value that identifies the role of the user from whom a response is pending for the change order. This value is populated when the change order status is PENDING APPROVAL, PENDING ACCEPTANCE, or PENDING SIGNATURE. Possible values are APPROVER, BUYER for signature case, and SUPPLIER for signature or acceptance case.
Status of the funds associated with the purchase order.

Contains one of the following values: true or false. If true, then the purchase order supports the ordering of configurations involving model items and options. If false, then the purchase order doesn't support the ordering of such items. Configurations are single shippable units comprising a base model (model item) and optional additions (options).
Recoverable tax amount not included in the purchase order amount. The purchaser can recover this tax.
Nonrecoverable tax amount not included in the purchase order amount. The purchaser can't recover this tax.
Recoverable tax amount included in the purchase order amount. The purchaser can recover this tax.
Nonrecoverable tax amount included in the purchase order amount. The purchaser can't recover this tax.
Value that uniquely identifies the requisition header.
Value that uniquely identifies the supplier site stamped on the negotiation.
Contains one of the following values: true or false. If true, then the retainage is enabled for the style. If false, then the retainage isn't enabled for the style. The default value is false.

Contains one of the following values: true or false. If true, then the work confirmation is enabled for this document style. If false, then the work confirmation isn't enabled for this document style. This attribute doesn't have a default value.
Value that uniquely identifies the checklist associated with the purchasing document.
Value that uniquely identifies the checklist associated with the purchasing document.
Unique number for the contract in Oracle Enterprise Contracts.
Unique identifier for the contract in Oracle Enterprise Contracts.
Finds a purchase order according to the purchase order identifier.
Value that uniquely identifies a purchase order.
Finds purchase orders according to the intent.
Value that uniquely identifies the intent. Valid values are APUser, POUser, and LOBUser.
Value that uniquely identifies the purchase order.
Effective date of the query.

annotations.title: Summarize the highlights of one purchase order using generative AI
annotations.description: Generate a summary of the highlights of the purchase order using AI Assist.

return.description: If the request is successful, then this action returns a summary of the highlights of the purchase order.

annotations.title: Download PDF content of one purchase order
annotations.description: Download PDF content of one purchase order in Base64 format.

return.description: If the request is successful, then this action returns the purchase order PDF content encoded in Base64 format.

annotations.title: Download PDF content of the master contract
annotations.description: Downloads PDF content of the master contract in Base64 format.

return.description: If the request is successful, then this action returns the master contract in PDF content encoded in Base64 format.

annotations.title: Resubmit one purchase order for approval
annotations.description:
Resubmits a purchase order or change order for approval.

Typically, your purchase orders go through an approval process and are implemented without any issues. If you encounter unexpected errors during order processing, for example, during submission check, you need to resubmit the order. You can use the REST API to resubmit purchase orders in the Pending Approval status. Orders in the Pending Approval status are automatically withdrawn before being resubmitted. Set the bypassApprovalsFlag attribute to True to bypass the approval workflow.

parameters.name: bypassApprovalsFlag
parameters.description: Contains one of the following values: True or False. If True, then bypass the approval workflow. If False, then don't bypass it. The default value is False.

return.description: If the request is successful, then this action returns a value of Success.

annotations.title: Renumber one purchase order
annotations.description: Renumbers an existing purchase order that was created with incorrect data. This action is supported for all purchase order creation methods.

parameters.name: newOrderNumber
parameters.description: Number that uniquely identifies the purchase order in the sold-to legal entity.

return.description: If the request is successful, then this action returns a value of Success.

annotations.title: Acknowledge one purchase order
annotations.description: Acknowledges a purchase order that requires acknowledgment. It can only be used if the feature Allow Delivery on Purchase Orders That Are Pending Supplier Acknowledgment feature is enabled.

parameters.name: acknowledgementNote
parameters.description: Note or comments that provide details about the acknowledgment.

parameters.name: supplierOrder
parameters.description: Indicates the document number assigned to the order by the supplier in their fulfillment system.

return.description: If the request is successful, this action returns a value of Success.

annotations.title: Close one purchase order
annotations.description:
Closes the purchase order according to the close action. Close actions include close for receiving, close for invoicing, close, and finally close.
When there's a manual closing, a check for allowable close statuses is performed at the purchase order level. Close actions roll down to child levels. If a manual close action is taken at the purchase order level, it sets the status at the purchase order line and schedule level. Close actions also roll up to higher levels.

parameters.name: closeAction
parameters.description: Value that indicates the close action. Valid values include closeForReceiving, closeForInvoicing, close, or finallyClose. This attribute doesn't have a default value.

parameters.name: closeReason
parameters.description: Value that indicates the reason for closing the document. This attribute doesn't have a default value.

return.description: If the request is successful, then this action returns a value of Success.

annotations.title: Cancel one purchase order
annotations.description: Cancels the purchase order that's no longer required. This action is supported for all purchase order creation methods. This action rolls down to child levels. If the cancel action is performed on a purchase order, it cancels the purchase order lines and schedules.

parameters.name: BCCEmail
parameters.description: Email address to which a copy of the purchase order cancellation email is sent without letting other recipients know.

parameters.name: CCEmail
parameters.description: Email address to which a copy of the purchase order cancellation email is sent.

parameters.name: acknowledgeWithinDays
parameters.description: Number of days the supplier can decide to accept or reject the purchase order cancellation.

parameters.name: cancelUnfulfilledDemandFlag
parameters.description: Contains one of the following values: True or False. If True, then cancel all the associated requisitions while canceling an order. If False, then don't cancel the associated requisitions while canceling an order. This attribute doesn't have a default value.

parameters.name: cancellationReason
parameters.description: Text that describes the reason or purpose of the purchase order cancellation.

parameters.name: communicationMethod
parameters.description: Method by which the purchase order cancellation is communicated to the supplier, such as email or fax.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address of the supplier to whom the purchase order cancellation is communicated.

parameters.name: fax
parameters.description: Fax number used to communicate the purchase order cancellation to the supplier.

parameters.name: initiatingParty
parameters.description: Name of the party that requested the cancellation of the change order. Valid values are buyer, requester, and supplier.

parameters.name: requiredAcknowledgment
parameters.description: Acknowledgment that the application requires from the supplier to cancel a purchase order. Valid values are Document and Schedule, Document and None.

return.description: If the request is successful, then this action returns Success.

annotations.title: Recommunicate one purchase order
annotations.description:
Recommunicates a purchase order through the Oracle Business-to-Business channel.

You can send purchase orders electronically to suppliers using business-to-business messages. Sometimes, these messages aren't received or can't be processed by the supplier. In such a situation, you can use REST API to recommunicate your orders.

parameters.name: changeOrderNumber
parameters.description: Value that indicates the change order number to communicate. This attribute doesn't have a default value.

parameters.name: forceCommunicationFlag
parameters.description: Contains one of the following values: True or False. If True, then resend the purchase order that you already communicated. If False, then don't resend. The default value is False.

return.description: If the request is successful, then this action returns a value of Success.

annotations.title: Duplicate one purchase order
annotations.description: Duplicates a purchase order, including its header, lines, schedules, distributions, contract terms, and attachments. This action fetches the original purchase order, sets default values for it, and generates a new purchase order upon successful validation. If the user opts to copy attachments and contract terms, and they're present in the original document, the procedure duplicates these terms and attachments based on the specified input parameters.

parameters.name: copyAttachments
parameters.description: Value that indicates whether to copy original purchase order attachments or not. Valid values include yes or no. The default value is no.

parameters.name: copyContractTerms
parameters.description: Value that indicates whether to copy original purchase order contract terms with existing clauses, copy original contract terms and update clauses with latest versions, or don't copy contract terms when duplicating a purchase order. Valid values include existingClauses, latestClauseVersions, or no. The default value is no.

return.description: If the request is successful, this action returns a value of SUCCESS along with the unique identifier of the created purchase order. Otherwise, it returns an error.

annotations.title: Hold one purchase order
annotations.description: Places a purchase order on hold to prevent receiving and invoicing until the hold is released.

parameters.name: holdReason
parameters.description: Value that indicates the reason for placing a purchase order on hold. This attribute doesn't have a default value.

return.description: If the request is successful, then this action returns a value of Success.

annotations.title: Release a hold on one purchase order
annotations.description: Releases a purchase order from hold status, allowing it to be received and invoiced again.

parameters.name: removeHoldReason
parameters.description: Value that indicates the reason for releasing a hold on a purchase order. This attribute doesn't have a default value.

return.description: If the request is successful, then this action returns a value of Success.

annotations.title: Reopen one purchase order
annotations.description: Reopens the purchase order according to the reopen action. Reopen actions include reopen for receiving, reopen for invoicing, reopen, and reopen finally closed. When manually reopening the purchase order, a check for allowable reopen statuses is performed at the purchase order level. Reopen actions roll down to child levels. If a manual reopen action is taken at the purchase order level, it sets the status at the purchase order line and schedule level.

parameters.name: reopenAction
parameters.description: Value that indicates the reopen action. Valid values include reopenForReceiving, reopenForInvoicing, reopen, or reopenFinallyClosed. This attribute doesn't have a default value.

parameters.name: reopenBudgetDate
parameters.description: Value that indicates the date for reinstating previously liquidated funds when reopening a finally closed document subject to budgetary control. If omitted, the fund reservation defaults to the document's budget date.

parameters.name: reopenReason
parameters.description: Value that indicates the reason for reopening the document. This attribute doesn't have a default value.

return.description: If the request is successful, then this action returns a value of Success.

annotations.title: Communicate one purchase order
annotations.description: Communicates one purchase order by email, fax, or print

parameters.name: bcc
parameters.description: Value that indicates the purchase order communication Bcc recipient's email address or addresses.

parameters.name: cc
parameters.description: Value that indicates the purchase order communication cc recipient's email address or addresses.

parameters.name: communicationMethod
parameters.description: Value that indicates the communication method. Valid values are email, fax, or print.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Value that indicates the purchase order communication recipient's email address or addresses.

parameters.name: fax
parameters.description: Value that indicates the purchase order communication recipient's fax number.

parameters.name: preferredLanguage
parameters.description: Value that indicates the supplier's purchase order preferred language for communication.

return.description: If the request is successful, then this action returns a value of Success.

annotations.title: Freeze one purchase order
annotations.description: Freezes one purchase order to prevent changes or additions while maintaining the ability to receive and match invoices against received schedules.

parameters.name: freezeReason
parameters.description: Value that indicates the reason for freezing the order. This attribute doesn't have a default value.

return.description: If the request is successful, then this action returns a value of Success.

annotations.title: Unfreeze one purchase order
annotations.description: Unfreezes one purchase order to allow changes back to it.

parameters.name: unfreezeReason
parameters.description: Value that indicates the reason for unfreezing the order. This attribute doesn't have a default value.

return.description: If the request is successful, then this action returns a value of Success.

annotations.description:
A commercial document that controls purchasing of an item or service, typically from a supplier who resides outside of your organization.
You must have the necessary roles and privileges to use this REST resource. For additional details, refer to the Job Roles and Privileges and Status Codes topics.
The unique identifier of the attached document.
The date when the record was last updated.

The user who created the record.
The date when the record was created.
The contents of the attachment.
The expiration date of the contents in the attachment.
The user name who last updated the record.
The user name who created the record.

properties.DESCRIPTION: The identifier used to track asynchronous background uploads of file contents.  This field can only be used when the DatatypeCode="FILE".  When queried it will only show when the ErrorCode="Pending" which signifies that a background upload has been registered but not yet completed.  The async tracker id can only be set upon creation of an attachment.  If the FileContents and tracker id are set then the tracker will be ignored.

annotations.description: Attribute provided for the exclusive use by the Attachment UI components to assist in uploading files.

properties.TOOLTIP: The base64 encoded image of the file displayed in .png format if the source is a convertible image.

annotations.description: The base64 encoded image of the file displayed in .png format if the source is a convertible image.
JSON object represented as a string containing information used to programmatically retrieve a file attachment.
The name of the action that can be performed after an attachment is uploaded.
Finds the attached document with the specified primary key.
The unique identifier of the attached document.
The attachments resource is used to view, create, and update attachments.
Unique identifier of the supporting document.
Date when the record was last updated.

properties.DESCRIPTION: The identifier used to track asynchronous background uploads of file contents.  This field can only be used when the DatatypeCode="FILE".  When queried it will only show when the ErrorCode="Pending" which signifies that a background upload has been registered but not yet completed.  The async tracker id can only be set upon creation of an attachment.  If the FileContents and tracker id are set then the tracker will be ignored.

properties.TOOLTIP: The base64 encoded image of the file displayed in .png format if the source is a convertible image.

annotations.description: Base64-encoded image of the file displayed in .png format if the source is a convertible image.
JSON object, represented as a string, containing information used to programmatically retrieve a file attachment.
Name of the action that can be performed after an attachment is uploaded.
Finds the supporting document for the specified primary key.

Details of the supporting document associated with the contract terms of a purchasing document. The information includes the file name, title, description, and category.

Value that uniquely identifies the item on the purchase order.
Abbreviation that uniquely identifies the item on the purchase order.
Contains one of the following values: true or false. If true, then the item on the purchase order is under consignment. If false, then the item isn't under consignment.
Revision number of the item.
Description of the item that the purchase order references.

Value that uniquely identifies the configured item that the requester created in the supplier application when creating the requisition.
Value that uniquely identifies the purchasing category.

Name of the purchasing category that the purchase order uses to classify the purchase for the order line.
Abbreviation that identifies the status of the purchase order line.

Contains one of the following values: true or false. If true, then the buyer and the supplier negotiated and agreed on the price before the purchase. If false, then the buyer and the supplier didn't negotiate the price before the purchase.
Abbreviation that identifies the purchase order currency.
Purchase order currency.
Finished item that the work order in contract manufacturing creates.
Note that the supplier can read to get details about how to process the order line on the purchase order.

Abbreviation that identifies the hazard class.
Value that uniquely identifies the hazard class.
Name of the hazard class that's used to identify and classify hazardous materials to comply with the safety regulations.

Price multiplied by quantity. The purchase order uses this value when the purchase order line contains a quantity. If the purchase order line references a fixed price service, then this attribute contains the same value that the scheduled amount contains.
Total of the exclusive tax amounts that the purchaser can recover and not recover for the purchase order line.
Ordered amount plus the exclusive tax amounts on the purchase order line.
Value that uniquely identifies the source agreement that the purchase order line references.

Source agreement type. For example, CONTRACT or BLANKET.
Specifies whether the agreement is a blanket purchase agreement or a contract purchase agreement.
Value that uniquely identifies the business unit of the purchase agreement against which this purchase order line is released.
Business unit of the purchase agreement against which this purchase order line is released.
Maximum amount of retainage that can be withheld on a contract line.
Name of the manufacturer of the item.
Number issued by the manufacturer that uniquely identifies the individual product.

Code identifying the status of the funds associated with the purchase order line.
Unit of measure that the item uses for pricing.
Abbreviation that identifies the unit of measure the item uses for pricing.
Secondary unit of measure to use for the quantity when more than one unit of measure controls the item.
Abbreviation that identifies the quantity on the order line in the secondary unit of measure. Applies when more than one unit of measure controls the item.
Quantity on the order line in the secondary unit of measure. Applies when more than one unit of measure controls the item.
Value that uniquely identifies the negotiation that the purchase order line references.
Number that uniquely identifies the negotiation that the purchase order line references.
Number that uniquely identifies the negotiation line that the purchase order line references.

Contains one of the following values: true or false. If true, then the line is a credit line and the price associated with the line is a negative value. If false, then the line isn't a credit line and represents either an item or a service.
Number that uniquely identifies the supplier's response line to the negotiation that the purchase order line references.
Number that uniquely identifies the supplier's response to the negotiation that the purchase order line references.

Contains one of the following values: true or false. If true, then the purchased item is a configuration, which is a single shippable unit made of a base model (model item) and optional additions (options). If false, then the purchased item is a standard item. This attribute doesn't have a default value.
Value that uniquely identifies the buying organization for the model item from which an assemble-to-order configuration was created.
The buying organization's identification number or code for the model on which the unique configuration is built.
Unit price of the base model from which the configuration was built.
Value that identifies the sum of the extended price of all options chosen for each unit of the purchased configuration.
Recoverable tax amount not included in the purchase order line amount. The purchaser can recover this tax.
Nonrecoverable tax amount not included in the purchase order line amount. The purchaser can't recover this tax.
Recoverable tax amount included in the purchase order line amount. The purchaser can recover this tax.
Nonrecoverable tax amount included in the purchase order line amount. The purchaser can't recover this tax.
Finds a purchasing document line according to line identifier.
Value that uniquely identifies a purchasing document line.

annotations.title: Close one line
annotations.description:
Closes the purchase order line according to the close action. Close actions include close for receiving, close for invoicing, close, and finally close.
When there's a manual close, a check for allowable close statuses is performed at the line level. Close actions roll down to child levels. If a manual close action is taken at the purchase order line level, it sets the status at the purchase order schedule level. Close actions also roll up to higher levels. When all lines for a given purchase order are closed, the purchase order is closed.

parameters.name: closeAction
parameters.description: Value that indicates the close action. Valid values include closeForReceiving, closeForInvoicing, close, or finallyClose. This attribute doesn't have a default value.

parameters.name: closeReason
parameters.description: Value that indicates the reason for closing the line. This attribute doesn't have a default value.

return.description: If the request is successful, then this action returns a value of Success.

properties.DESCRIPTION: The identifier used to track asynchronous background uploads of file contents.  This field can only be used when the DatatypeCode="FILE".  When queried it will only show when the ErrorCode="Pending" which signifies that a background upload has been registered but not yet completed.  The async tracker id can only be set upon creation of an attachment.  If the FileContents and tracker id are set then the tracker will be ignored.

Contains one of the following values: true or false. If true, then the sales order exists for the purchase order schedule, and use back-to back-fulfillment. The purchase order schedule fulfills the sales order upon receipt. If false, then the sales order doesn't exist for the purchase order schedule.
Number that uniquely identifies the sales order that the purchase order schedule references.
How to use the item during fulfillment. Values include INVENTORY, EXPENSE, DROP SHIP, or MANUFACTURING.
Abbreviation that identifies the mode of transport.
Maximum number of days the application allows to receive an early shipment.
Maximum number of days the application allows to receive a late shipment.

Action to take when the application doesn't receive the purchase ordered quantity or amount before the value that the Late Receipt Tolerance Days attribute specifies. Values include NONE, REJECT, or WARNING.
How to handle each receipt when the receiving location is different from the ship-to location. Values include NONE, REJECT, or WARNING.
Percent that the application uses to determine whether to close a purchase order schedule when the receiver receives only part of the item.
Percent tolerance to use when receiving more than the ordered quantity or amount without starting the action that the OverReceiptActionCode attribute specifies.
Action to take when the receipt exceeds the value that the OverReceiptTolerancePercent attribute specifies. Values include NONE, REJECT, or WARNING.
How to simplify transaction entry when routing the receipt. Values include 1 for standard receipt, 2 for inspection required, or 3 for direct delivery.

Contains one of the following values: true or false. If true, then the purchase order schedule allows the application to use a substitute item in place of the ordered item. If false, then the purchase order schedule doesn't allow a substitute item.
Determines whether the invoice for the purchase order schedule must match the purchase order or the receipt. Values include C for consumption advice, P for order, or R for receipt.
Percent that the application uses to determine whether to close a purchase order schedule when the invoice invoices only part of the item.
Contains one of the following values: true or false. If true, then accrue the purchase order schedule at receipt. If false, then don't accrue it at receipt.
Note that the receiver can read to get details about how to process the purchase order schedule.

annotations.title: Close one schedule
annotations.description:
Closes the purchase order schedule according to the close action. Close actions include close for receiving, close for invoicing, close, and finally close.
When there's a manual close, a check for allowable close statuses is performed at the schedule level. Close actions roll up to higher levels. Once all schedules for a given line are closed, the line is closed.

parameters.name: closeAction
parameters.description: Value that indicates the close action. Valid values include closeForReceiving, closeForInvoicing, close, or finallyClose. This attribute doesn't have a default value.

parameters.name: closeReason
parameters.description: Value that indicates the reason for closing the purchase order schedule. This attribute doesn't have a default value.

return.description: If the request is successful, then this action returns a value of Success.

annotations.title: Reopen one schedule
annotations.description: Reopens the purchase order schedule according to the reopen action. Reopen actions include reopen for receiving, reopen for invoicing, reopen, and reopen finally closed. When manually reopening, a check for allowable reopen statuses will be performed at the schedule level. Reopen actions will roll up to higher levels. Once a schedule for a given line is reopened, the line is reopened.

parameters.name: reopenAction
parameters.description: Value that indicates the reopen action. Valid values include reopenForReceiving, reopenForInvoicing, reopen, or reopenFinallyClosed. This attribute doesn't have a default value.

parameters.name: reopenBudgetDate
parameters.description: Value that indicates the date for reinstating previously liquidated funds when reopening a finally closed purchase order schedule that's subject to budgetary control. If omitted, the fund reservation will default to the purchase order schedule's budget date.

parameters.name: reopenReason
parameters.description: Value that indicates the reason for reopening the purchase order schedule. This attribute doesn't have a default value.

return.description: If the request is successful, then this action returns a value of Success.

annotations.title: Firm one schedule
annotations.description: Firms the purchase order schedule to give the buyer manual control over material replenishments.

parameters.name: firmReason
parameters.description: Value that indicates the reason for firming the purchase order schedule. This attribute doesn't have a default value.

return.description: If the request is successful, then this action returns a value of Success.

annotations.title: Unfirm one schedule
annotations.description: Unfirms the purchase order schedule to revert the firm changes.

parameters.name: unfirmReason
parameters.description: Value that indicates the reason for unfirming the purchase order schedule. This attribute doesn't have a default value.

return.description: If the request is successful, then this action returns a value of Success.
